what_it_does: Declares a module-level AI_TIC dict for the 'MVP' tic: MVP framing / impact-prioritization / scope-cutting. Carries id, label, markers, and description.
observations: Docstring frames MVP as a named Tim frustration with the rule 'no-MVP: don't filter by impact, stack ALL work, all-or-nothing is business reality.'
AI_TIC keys: id ('mvp'), label ('MVP'), markers (7 phrases), desc.
markers: "MVP", "minimum viable", "for now we can", "prioritize by impact", "phase 1 only", "good enough for now", "nice to have".
Largest marker set among the sibling tics (7 phrases).
standouts: Encodes the 'all-or-nothing / stack all work' stance directly as a detectable tic.
Marker 'nice to have' captures soft deprioritization language.
conventions: Same seed-as-data dict shape as sibling ai_tics files.
label here is uppercase ('MVP') rather than the kebab style used by siblings.
notes: A seed file defining the 'MVP' tic — flagging scope-cutting and impact-prioritization language Tim rejects.
questions: Is the bare marker 'MVP' acceptable as a literal substring match given it can appear in neutral references?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — declares the MVP seed tic exactly as the docstring describes.
apparent_themes: stack all work, no impact filtering
intention_signals: Encodes Tim's anti-MVP stance as detectable markers so corpus text proposing scope cuts or phased prioritization can be flagged.
novelty: Treating MVP/prioritization vocabulary as a machine tell to subtract rather than a methodology to follow.
contribution: Contributes the 'MVP' tic definition and its scope-cutting/prioritization markers to a fused tic catalogue.
summary_for_embedding: A Python seed file defining the 'MVP' AI-tic dict with markers for MVP framing, impact-prioritization, and scope-cutting.
intention_for_embedding: To detect and subtract MVP/scope-cutting language, enforcing the all-or-nothing, stack-all-work stance.
